stem: A data set consists of the five values [[x1]], [[x2]], [[x3]], [[x4]], [[x5]]. What is the mean of this data set?
solution: The mean is the sum of the values divided by how many there are: \( \dfrac{[[x1]]+[[x2]]+[[x3]]+[[x4]]+[[x5]]}{5} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This divides by 4 instead of the correct count of 5 values.
This divides by 6 instead of the correct count of 5 values.
This is the sum of the values without dividing by the count at all.
This is just the third listed value, not the average of all five.
This averages only the first and last listed values, ignoring the other three.
This is off by one from the correct mean.

stem: A data set consists of the five values [[x1]], [[x2]], [[x3]], [[x4]], [[x5]]. What is the median of this data set?
solution: Sorted, the five values are \( [[x2]] < [[x1]] < [[x3]] < [[x4]] < [[x5]] \) or a similar ordering with two values below and two above [[x3]]; either way the middle value is \( [[x3]] = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the MEAN of the data set, not the median.
This is one of the values below the median, not the middle value.
This is one of the values below the median, not the middle value.
This is one of the values above the median, not the middle value.
This is one of the values above the median, not the middle value.
This is off by one from the correct median.

stem: A data set consists of the five values [[lo]], [[mid1]], [[mid2]], [[mid3]], [[hi]]. What is the range of this data set (the difference between the maximum and minimum values)?
solution: The maximum is [[hi]] and the minimum is [[lo]], so the range is \( [[hi]]-[[lo]] = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This adds the maximum and minimum instead of subtracting them.
This uses two interior values instead of the true maximum and minimum.
This uses an interior value instead of the true minimum.
This halves the correct range for no valid reason.
This uses an interior value instead of the true minimum.
This is off by one from the correct range.

stem: A population data set consists of the four values [[x1]], [[x2]], [[x3]], [[x4]], with mean \( [[mbar]] \). What is the population variance of this data set (dividing the summed squared deviations from the mean by \( n \), not \( n-1 \))?
solution: The population variance divides the summed squared deviations from \( \bar{x}=[[mbar]] \) by \( n=4 \): \( \dfrac{[[ssq]]}{4} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This divides by \( n-1=3 \); that is the unbiased SAMPLE variance, not the population variance asked for here.
This is the total of the squared deviations without dividing at all.
This takes the square root, giving the standard deviation instead of the variance.
This divides by 2 instead of the correct count \( n=4 \).
This is off by one from the correct population variance.
This is off by one from the correct population variance.

stem: A population data set consists of the four values [[x1]], [[x2]], [[x3]], [[x4]]. The population mean is [[m]]. What is the population standard deviation (the square root of the variance, dividing by \( n \))?
solution: Take the square root of the population variance: \( s = \sqrt{\dfrac{(-[[k]])^2+(-[[k]])^2+[[k]]^2+[[k]]^2}{4}} = \sqrt{[[k]]^2} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This divides by \( n-1=3 \) inside the square root; that gives the unbiased sample standard deviation, not the population one.
This is the population variance itself, without taking the square root.
This divides by 5 instead of the correct count \( n=4 \).
This is off by one from the correct standard deviation.
This is off by one from the correct standard deviation.
This takes the square root of the sum of squared RAW values instead of squared deviations from the mean.

stem: A data set consists of the five values [[m]], [[c1]], [[m]], [[c2]], [[m]]. What is the mode of this data set (the value that occurs most frequently)?
solution: The value [[m]] occurs three times while [[c1]] and [[c2]] each occur once, so the mode is \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This value occurs only once, not most frequently.
This value occurs only once, not most frequently.
This is the MEAN of the data set, not the mode.
This is off by one from the correct mode.
This is off by one from the correct mode.
This is an arbitrary combination of the other values, not the most frequent one.
